sections.instruction: Questions 1-6: Complete the form below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 7-10: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.content: Coordinator: Gary {{BLANK}}
Level: {{BLANK}}
Place: the {{BLANK}}
{{BLANK}} Street first floor, room T347
Time: Thursday morning at {{BLANK}}
Recommended website: The perfect {{BLANK}}
Complete the table below. {{BLANK}}

sections.instruction: Questions 11-16: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 17 and 18: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Questions 19 and 20: Choose TWO letters, A-E.

questions.content: What made David leave London and move to Northsea?
options: A He was eager to develop a hobby.
B He wanted to work shorter hours.
C He found his job in website design unsatisfying.

questions.content: The Lifeboat Institution in Northsea was built with money provided by
options: A a local organisation.
B a local resident.
C the local council.

questions.content: In his health assessment, the doctor was concerned about the fact that David
options: A might be colour blind.
B was rather short-sighted.
C had undergone eye surgery.

questions.content: After arriving at the lifeboat station, they aim to launch the boat within
options: A five minutes.
B six to eight minutes.
C eight and a half minutes.

questions.content: As a ‘helmsman’, David has the responsibility of deciding
options: A who will be the members of his crew.
B what equipment it will be necessary to take.
C if the lifeboat should be launched.

questions.content: As well as going out on the lifeboat, David
options: A gives talks on safety at sea.
B helps with fundraising.
C recruits new volunteers.

questions.content: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?
options: A The residential course developed his leadership skills.
B The training in use of ropes and knots was quite brief.
C The training exercises have built up his mental strength.
D The casualty care activities were particularly challenging for him.
E The wave tank activities provided practice in survival techniques.

questions.content: Which TWO things does David find most motivating about the work he does?
options: A working as part of a team
B experiences when working in winter
C being thanked by those he has helped
D the fact that it keeps him fit
E the chance to develop new equipment

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 25-28: Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 25-28. Questions 29-30: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.content: At first, Don thought the topic of recycling footwear might be too
options: A limited in scope.
B hard to research.
C boring for listeners.

questions.content: When discussing trainers, Bella and Don disagree about
options: A how popular they are among young people.
B how suitable they are for school.
C how quickly they wear out.

questions.content: Bella says that she sometimes recycles shoes because
options: A they no longer fit.
B she no longer likes them.
C they are no longer in fashion.

questions.content: What did the article say that confused Don?
options: A Public consumption of footwear has risen.
B Less footwear is recycled now than in the past.
C People dispose of more footwear than they used to.

questions.content: What reasons did the recycling manager give for rejecting footwear, according to the students? Match the reason to each item of footwear: 25. the high-heeled shoes 26. the ankle boots 27. the baby shoes 28. the trainers
options: A one shoe was missing
B the colour of one shoe had faded
C one shoe had a hole in it
D the shoes were brand new
E the shoes were too dirty
F the stitching on the shoes was broken

questions.content: Why did the project to make ‘new’ shoes out of old shoes fail?
options: A People believed the ‘new’ pairs of shoes were unhygienic.
B There were not enough good parts to use in the old shoes.
C The shoes in the ’new’ pairs were not completely alike.

questions.content: Bella and Don agree that they can present their topic
options: A from a new angle.
B with relevant images.
C in a straightforward way.

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Tardigrades
• more than 1,000 species, 0.05-1.2 millimetres long

questions.content: also known as water ‘bears’ (due to how they {{BLANK}}) and ‘moss piglets’
Physical appearance: a {{BLANK}} round body and four pairs of legs
Physical appearance: claws or {{BLANK}} for gripping
Physical appearance: body filled with a liquid that carries both {{BLANK}} and blood
Physical appearance: mouth shaped like a {{BLANK}} with teeth called stylets
Habitat: very resilient and can exist in very low or high {{BLANK}}
Cryptobiosis: A type of {{BLANK}} ensures their DNA is not damaged.
Cryptobiosis: Research is underway to find out how many days they can stay alive in {{BLANK}}
Feeding: consume liquids, e.g., those found in moss or {{BLANK}}
Conservation status: They are not considered to be {{BLANK}}
